title: calathea altissima
intro: Calathea altissima, also known as the 'Medusa' or 'Medusa's Head' plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, characterized by its unique, curly foliage that resembles the mythological Medusa's snakes. Traditionally, the Calathea altissima plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly in South America, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and roots are believed to have various health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues, among others. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the Calathea altissima plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
